items.final_wording: Supports documenting pressure-ulcer surface area, validated measurement or photography, estimated depth, undermining, clinician-assigned category, and repeat assessment without automatically calculating or assigning a category.

source.title: Pressure ulcers: prevention and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: Adults, children, young people, infants, and neonates at risk of or with a pressure ulcer.
source.setting: Primary, community, emergency, secondary, and tertiary care pressure-risk and wound assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting pressure-ulcer risk factors, skin assessment, ulcer surface area, depth, undermining, category, photography, and repeated assessment. It does not generate a risk score, diagnosis, treatment, dressing, offloading, referral, or urgency.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting pressure-ulcer surface area, validated measurement or photography, estimated depth, undermining, clinician-assigned category, and repeat assessment without automatically calculating or assigning a category.

items.rationale: The exact measurement section supports documenting a pressure-ulcer follow-up, interval change, function and symptom impact, clinician skin inspection, site or distribution, photography and repeated assessment without automatically measuring, categorising, or diagnosing the ulcer.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting mobility, previous or current pressure ulcer, nutritional status, cognition, sensory impairment, posture, deformity, skin integrity, colour change, heat, firmness, moisture, and pain only when assessed.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting pressure-ulcer surface area, validated measurement or photography, estimated depth, undermining, clinician-assigned category, and repeat assessment without automatically calculating or assigning a category.

added_items.rationale: The exact measurement section supports documenting a pressure-ulcer follow-up, interval change, function and symptom impact, clinician skin inspection, site or distribution, photography and repeated assessment without automatically measuring, categorising, or diagnosing the ulcer.

added_items.final_wording: Supports documenting mobility, previous or current pressure ulcer, nutritional status, cognition, sensory impairment, posture, deformity, skin integrity, colour change, heat, firmness, moisture, and pain only when assessed.

source.title: Pressure ulcers: prevention and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: Adults, children, young people, infants, and neonates at risk of or with a pressure ulcer.
source.setting: Primary, community, emergency, secondary, and tertiary care pressure-risk and wound assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting pressure-ulcer risk factors, skin assessment, ulcer surface area, depth, undermining, category, photography, and repeated assessment. It does not generate a risk score, diagnosis, treatment, dressing, offloading, referral, or urgency.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.7 — adult risk and skin assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting mobility, previous or current pressure ulcer, nutritional status, cognition, sensory impairment, posture, deformity, skin integrity, colour change, heat, firmness, moisture, and pain only when assessed.

added_items.rationale: The exact skin-assessment section supports recording pain, general condition and relevant risk context only when assessed.
